The Global Network for Sri Lankan Founders

Build something
the world can't ignore

From Seattle to Singapore, London to Colombo — Sri Lankan founders are building world-class companies on every continent. Ceylon Ventures connects you with global investors, mentors, and each other. Wherever you're building, this is your platform.

🔒 No investments flow through Ceylon Ventures — ever. All transactions are directly between parties.
✓ Open to Sri Lankan founders in every country
✓ Connect with global investors directly
✓ Zero commission. Ever.
Sri Lankan founders are building in 50+ countries — these are the global companies they're aiming to join
By the numbers

The opportunity in global venture

Global venture is bigger than ever. AI is reshaping every industry. Capital is flowing into early-stage companies at record pace — and founders from underrepresented communities are increasingly building category-defining businesses. Ceylon Ventures plugs Sri Lankan founders directly into this global flow.

Sources: PitchBook Q1 2026 · Hurun Global Unicorn Index 2026 · Dealroom · NVCA 2025 Yearbook

$0T
Combined valuation of 1,680 global unicorn companies — and the next ones are being built now

Why we exist

Sri Lankan founders are building
everywhere on earth.
Now there's one place to find them.

For decades, Sri Lankan ambition has scaled across continents while opportunity stayed fragmented. Founders raise locally, mentors operate regionally, capital concentrates by geography — and the collective potential of a globally distributed community remains underutilized.

Ceylon Ventures consolidates that potential into a single platform. One verified network where founders, investors, operators, and mentors transact, connect, and compound — irrespective of where any of them are based. This is the infrastructure layer the Sri Lankan economy has been missing.

🇱🇰 Colombo🇬🇧 London🇨🇦 Toronto 🇸🇬 Singapore🇦🇺 Sydney🇦🇪 Dubai 🇺🇸 San Francisco+ 44 more cities
How it works

From profile to partnership

✦
STEP 01
Build your verified profile

List your startup and tell your story. AI tools help you craft the pitch that lands — from narrative to market sizing. Get verified from just $19.99/year.

◎
STEP 02
Get discovered globally

Our matching engine surfaces you to the right investors worldwide — by sector, stage, geography, and thesis. Promote milestones to a growing audience.

→
STEP 03
Connect directly — no gatekeepers

Direct chat. No intermediaries. No commission. Whether you're raising your first round or Series A, everything after the introduction happens between you and your investor.

◈
STEP 01
Set your thesis

Tell us what you back — sectors, stages, check sizes. Takes two minutes. We use this to build your personalised dealflow from day one.

⬡
STEP 02
Receive curated dealflow

Pre-vetted Sri Lankan startups matched to your thesis. Browse risk scores, traction, and founder stories — all before first contact.

⟳
STEP 03
Connect and move

Reach founders directly. Run diligence with our resource library. All terms, agreements, and capital flow entirely between you and the founder.

For Founders

Start building something that matters

Building a company in Seattle? London? Singapore? Toronto? If you're a Sri Lankan founder anywhere in the world, Ceylon Ventures is your platform. Your location is not a barrier — it's your superpower. You bring the global perspective. We bring the network and capital connections.

  • ✓Verified founder profile — The trust signal that gets the first meeting
  • ✓Post your business idea — Share concepts, attract early believers and co-founders
  • ✓Direct chat with global investors — No middlemen, no commission
  • ✓AI pitch builder & valuation tools — Craft pitches that land, with data to back them
  • ✓Founder Story Studio — Narratives investors actually remember
  • ✓Social media promotion — Company, milestones, products promoted to our audience
  • ✓Co-founder & talent matching — Find the people who'll build it with you

For Investors

Sri Lankan founders, building globally — access them all

Any investor, anywhere in the world. Sri Lankan founders are building in San Francisco, Seattle, London, Singapore and beyond — and they're raising from global investors. Ceylon Ventures is where you find them first, across every sector and every geography, at the earliest stage.

  • ✓Curated dealflow — Vetted startups across AI, fintech, agritech, climate
  • ✓AI thesis matching — Personalised recommendations, not a raw directory
  • ✓Risk scoring & analytics — Make informed decisions faster
  • ✓Due diligence library — Everything needed to evaluate an opportunity
  • ✓Direct founder access — Chat directly. Zero commission.
Early investors target 20–30% IRR vs. ~8% in public markets.* Ceylon Ventures facilitates connections only. All investment decisions and transactions are made independently between parties. *Illustrative only. Early-stage investing carries significant risk of loss.

Why Sri Lankan Founders

A global community
that punches above its weight

The Sri Lankan community is 1.3 million strong across 50+ countries — highly educated, deeply networked, and building companies at the cutting edge of every industry. The domestic Sri Lanka ecosystem has grown 3× in 2.5 years. And the global Sri Lankan wealth network represents $20–30B in deployable capital looking for exactly these opportunities.

Building the future
Our Mission
This isn't a startup platform.
It's a 100-year movement.

We believe that where you were born shouldn't determine what you can build. A Sri Lankan founder in Seattle deserves the same network, capital access, and support as a founder in Silicon Valley. Ceylon Ventures is the infrastructure that makes that true.

Access
Open the door

Direct entry to the private marketplace once reserved for the privileged few. Every Sri Lankan founder deserves a seat at the global table.

Ownership
Build equity

Turning a nation of employees into a generation of owners — building and owning equity in startups, global companies, and intellectual property.

Connection
Bridge worlds

Bridging international capital with local talent, global markets with Sri Lankan innovation, experienced operators with ambitious founders — across every continent.

"The richest Sri Lankans in history haven't been born yet.
We're building the ecosystem that will create them."
About Us

Building a globally connected
innovation ecosystem

Ceylon Ventures is a global venture platform built to accelerate the rise of Sri Lankan founders shaping the future of business, technology, and innovation.

We exist to identify, support, and elevate visionary entrepreneurs with the ambition to build category-defining companies on a global scale. Through strategic capital, international networks, media, and ecosystem development, Ceylon Ventures creates pathways for Sri Lankan innovation to compete beyond borders.

Our platform brings together founders, investors, creators, and operators who believe the next generation of transformative companies can emerge from emerging markets and stand alongside the world's leading businesses.

Driven by long-term thinking and a global perspective, we focus on ventures with the potential to redefine industries, create meaningful impact, and inspire the next era of entrepreneurship.

At Ceylon Ventures, we are not simply building companies — we are building a globally connected innovation ecosystem for the future of Sri Lankan entrepreneurship.

Important Notice: Ceylon Ventures is a connection and discovery platform only. We do not facilitate, process, or hold investment transactions, and we take no commission. All investment transactions, equity transfers, and legal agreements occur directly between founders and investors outside the platform, in compliance with applicable securities regulations in each jurisdiction. © 2026 Ceylon Ventures, Inc. All rights reserved. · Headquartered in Seattle, Washington, USA.
Not Investment Advice

Nothing on this platform constitutes investment advice, financial advice, trading advice, or any other form of advice. Ceylon Ventures, Inc. does not recommend that any security, investment, or financial instrument should be bought, sold, or held by any specific person. All information provided is for informational and connection purposes only.

Risk of Loss

Investing in early-stage companies involves a high degree of risk. You may lose some or all of your investment. Past performance of any deal, company, or investment referenced on this platform is not indicative of future results. Valuations are estimates and may not reflect the actual or future value of any company.

Accredited Investors Only

Access to deal information on this platform is restricted to accredited investors, sophisticated investors, or professional investors as defined under the applicable laws of their jurisdiction. This includes but is not limited to definitions under the U.S. Securities Act of 1933 (Regulation D), the UK Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (FSMA), the EU Prospectus Regulation, the MAS Securities and Futures Act (Singapore), the DFSA regulations (UAE), the FCA rules (United Kingdom), the ASIC Corporations Act (Australia), and equivalent legislation in all other applicable jurisdictions. By accessing deal materials, you represent and warrant that you meet the eligibility requirements under the laws of your country of residence.

No Public Offering

Information presented on this platform does not constitute a public offering of securities in any jurisdiction. Any securities described have not been registered under any securities laws and may not be offered or sold in jurisdictions where registration or qualification is required without an applicable exemption.

Regulatory Status

Ceylon Ventures, Inc. is not a registered broker-dealer, investment adviser, or financial institution. We do not hold client funds or execute trades on behalf of users. Users are responsible for conducting their own due diligence and obtaining independent legal, tax, and financial advice before making any investment decision.

Global Access & Jurisdiction

This platform is accessible globally but is not intended for use in jurisdictions where such access would be contrary to applicable law or regulation. Users are solely responsible for ensuring their use of this platform complies with all laws, regulations, and restrictions applicable in their country of residence or domicile. Ceylon Ventures, Inc. makes no representation that the content of this platform is appropriate or available for use in any particular jurisdiction.

© 2026 Ceylon Ventures, Inc.

Knowledge Center

Everything a founder or investor actually needs to know

Straight-talking guides on fundraising, term sheets, cap tables, and every acronym investors casually drop in first meetings. Written for Sri Lankan founders and investors — with a global perspective, without the American VC assumptions.

🔍

Popular: SAFEs · Angel investing 101 · Seed rounds · Term sheets

Featured guide

The essentials

If you're new to fundraising or investing, start here. These are the foundational concepts every founder and investor needs to know cold — before signing anything, before wiring anything.

Foundational · 15 min read

Angel Investing 101: A guide for both sides of the table

What angels actually do, how they think about risk, what a typical check looks like, and how founders should structure their first outreach. Built from real conversations with Sri Lankan operators-turned-angels investing globally in 2026.

By Ceylon Ventures Editorial Updated Q2 2026 15 min read

What you'll learn

  • How angels evaluate deals in under 30 minutes
  • Check-size norms: $1K to $250K explained
  • The difference between angel, syndicate, and VC
  • Legal structures: SAFE vs convertible vs equity
  • What "warm intros" actually mean in practice

Fundraising instruments

Every legal structure founders and investors need to know
Legal · Instrument

SAFEs (Simple Agreement for Future Equity)

The default fundraising instrument for early-stage startups since Y Combinator introduced it in 2013. A SAFE converts to equity in a future priced round. No maturity date. No interest. One clean document, one negotiable term (the valuation cap).

12 min read Founders · Investors →
Legal · Instrument

Convertible Notes

The predecessor to the SAFE. A convertible note is debt that converts to equity — with a maturity date, interest rate, and (usually) a valuation cap or discount. Still widely used, especially outside the US where SAFEs are less recognized.

10 min read Founders · Investors →
Legal · Instrument

Preferred Stock (Priced Rounds)

The "real" equity round. When your Series A investor wires their check, they buy Preferred Stock — a share class with liquidation preferences, anti-dilution rights, and board seats. This guide explains every clause on the term sheet, in plain English.

18 min read Founders · Investors →
Finance · Debt

Revenue-Share Agreements

Also called "revenue-based financing" or RBF. Investor gives you capital; you pay back a fixed multiple (e.g. 1.5x) as a percentage of monthly revenue until you hit the cap. No dilution. No board. Best for cash-flow-positive businesses with predictable revenue.

8 min read Founders →
Finance · Debt

Simple Loans & Working Capital

Straight debt. You borrow, you pay back with interest, no equity involved. When it makes sense (short bridge, inventory financing, receivables), when it doesn't (funding losses, building runway), and how to negotiate terms as a startup.

7 min read Founders →
Finance · Advanced

Private Credit

The $1.7T asset class that has quietly become one of the largest sources of capital for growth-stage companies. What private credit is, how it differs from venture debt and revenue financing, and when founders should (and shouldn't) reach for it.

14 min read Investors · Later-stage founders →

Rounds & stages

What each round means, when to raise it, and what to expect
Founders · Fundraising

The Seed Round Explained

What a seed round actually is in 2026 (spoiler: it's much bigger than it used to be). Median seed rounds are now $3M globally. When you're ready. Who leads. How to price it. And why the "seed" and "pre-seed" distinction still matters even though everyone pretends it doesn't.

16 min read Founders →
Founders · Fundraising

Pre-Seed: The First Capital

Before institutional VCs. Before named angel groups. When it's just you, a deck, and maybe a prototype. How Sri Lankan founders successfully raise $50K–$500K from friends, family, and first-check angels — without giving away too much of the company.

11 min read Founders →
Founders · Fundraising

Series A: The Growth Round

The round where the game gets serious. Now expected: $1M+ ARR, real traction, a proper board seat, and full priced-round documentation. Median Series A globally in 2025 was $12M. What VCs actually look for, and how to run a proper process.

20 min read Founders →
Founders · Fundraising

Bridge Rounds & Extensions

What to do when you're between rounds. When a bridge signals strength (accelerating) vs. weakness (running out of money). How to price them, who to raise from, and how not to trigger down-round protections that destroy your cap table.

9 min read Founders →
Founders · Community

Crowdfunding & Community Rounds

How platforms like Wefunder, Republic, and Crowdcube let founders raise from customers and community. When it's brilliant marketing plus capital. When it's a distraction. Legal frameworks in the US (Reg CF, Reg A+), UK (FCA), Singapore (MAS), and EU.

13 min read Founders →
Cross-Border

Global Raises for Sri Lankan Founders

The specific playbook for a Sri Lankan founder building in Seattle, London, or Singapore who wants to raise from investors on multiple continents. Corporate structures (Delaware C-corp vs Cayman vs UK Ltd), tax implications, and which structure investors expect at each stage.

22 min read Founders · Cross-border →

Investor vehicles

How money actually gets pooled and deployed
Investors · Foundational

Angel Investing 101

The complete guide. What angels do differently from VCs. How to build a portfolio when 90% of startups fail. Check-size norms ($1K–$250K), diligence in under an hour, and how the best angels source deals that VCs never see. Free to read; free to reference forever.

15 min read Investors →
Investors · Structures

SPVs (Special Purpose Vehicles)

How lead investors pool many small checks from angels into a single line on the founder's cap table. Legal structure, cost, tax pass-through, and when platforms like AngelList, Sydecar, or Assure make sense vs. rolling your own LLC.

11 min read Investors · Syndicate leads →
Investors · Structures

Syndicates

How a lead investor with reputation and dealflow lets other angels invest alongside them. Carry (typically 20%), management fees, and the informal-but-real trust relationship that makes syndicates work — or fail.

9 min read Investors →
Investors · Advanced

Venture Funds Explained

How a VC fund is actually structured. LPs, GPs, management fees (2%), carry (20%), fund vintages, and why funds have a 10-year life. Also: rolling funds, evergreen funds, and micro-VCs — the newer structures reshaping early-stage.

17 min read Investors · Aspiring GPs →
Investors · Portfolio

Portfolio Construction

How many companies to back, average check size, follow-on strategy, and the math of power-law returns. Why professional angels invest in 20+ companies per fund cycle. Reserves for follow-ons and when to double down.

13 min read Investors →
Investors · Advanced

Secondaries

How to buy shares from existing shareholders — founders, early employees, or exiting angels — instead of buying new equity from the company. When secondaries make sense, how they're priced, and the growing secondaries market post-2024.

10 min read Investors · Later-stage →

Term sheets & legal

Every clause. Every acronym. Plain English.
Legal · Reference

The Term Sheet Decoded

Every line item in a real term sheet, explained. Liquidation preferences (1x non-participating vs 2x participating), anti-dilution (broad-based vs full ratchet), pro-rata rights, drag-along, tag-along, board composition, and vesting acceleration.

25 min read Founders · Investors →
Legal · Foundational

The Cap Table

What your cap table is, why every founder should know theirs cold, and how to model it from day one. Common vs preferred, options pool, SAFEs on the pre-money vs post-money side, and how dilution actually happens over 3 rounds.

14 min read Founders →
Legal · Foundational

Founder Vesting & ESOPs

4-year vesting with a 1-year cliff. What that actually means. Why co-founders need it (yes, even if you're best friends). ESOP pool sizing (10–15% typical), option pricing (409A valuations), and how to make sure your first 20 hires actually own something meaningful.

12 min read Founders →
Legal · Cross-Border

Delaware Flip & Corporate Structures

If you're a Sri Lankan founder, when and how do you incorporate in Delaware? Or should you use a Singapore Pte Ltd? A UK Ltd? The tradeoffs by tax treaty, investor expectation, and IP protection. Includes a decision flowchart.

18 min read Founders · Cross-border →
Legal · Reference

Valuation Caps & Discounts

The two most misunderstood terms in early-stage fundraising. What a "cap" actually means for the investor's ownership. When discounts (usually 20%) matter more. What happens when your round closes above the cap — and why founders lose sleep over it.

10 min read Founders · Investors →
Legal · Reference

NDAs, Founder Agreements & IP Assignment

Before you take a single dollar: split your equity, agree who owns what, and make sure your IP is assigned to the company. Every founder war-story that ends "and now we're in litigation" started with someone skipping this step.

8 min read Founders →

Deep dives

Specialist topics, written for the moment you need them
Cross-Border

Cross-Border Cap Tables

You're a Sri Lankan founder in the UK, US investors on your cap table, Sri Lankan angels holding SAFEs, and a Singapore holding company. This is the modern reality. How to model it, how to file it, and how to keep it clean through the next 3 rounds.

19 min read Founders · Advanced →
Investors · Foundational

Reading Startup Financials

ARR vs MRR. Gross margin vs contribution margin. Net dollar retention. Cash runway vs burn multiple. The 10 metrics every startup investor should ask about — and the 5 that founders will try to hide.

16 min read Investors →
Investors · Advanced

Due Diligence Playbook

What sophisticated angels actually check before writing a check. Reference calls, product usage checks, cap table sanity, market sizing challenges, and legal DD. A downloadable checklist included.

14 min read Investors →
Free templates

Documents you can actually use

Downloadable templates, modeled on YC's SAFE library and industry-standard forms. Every document reviewed by counsel in the jurisdictions listed. Free — always.

📄

SAFE templates (Post-Money)

The three standard variants — valuation cap only, discount only, and MFN. Modeled on YC's post-money SAFE, adapted for use by non-US Sri Lankan founders. Includes Delaware, Singapore, and Cayman versions.

DOCSAFE — Valuation Cap Only28 KB↓ DOCSAFE — Discount Only26 KB↓ DOCSAFE — MFN Uncapped24 KB↓ PDFSAFE User Guide1.2 MB↓
📊

Cap table & financial models

Google Sheets templates you can copy and modify. Model your dilution across 3 rounds, forecast your runway, and build a proper ESOP pool budget — before your investor asks for it.

Founder documents

The essential pre-fundraising documents. Founder agreements. IP assignment. Vesting schedules. All templates a Sri Lankan founder needs to get right before taking their first outside check.

Pitch deck templates
